name: Snow Report 2026-03-09 05:46:08
report:
Good morning, Pico family,

We had another great day on the hill yesterday with soft skiing and riding under plenty of sunshine. We lucked out with pretty good conditions this weekend, about as nice a kickoff to the spring season as you could ask for.

Sunny skies are back today with temperatures climbing to the low-to-mid 40s. However, it’s going to be quite a breezy one with southwest winds gusting up to 50 mph at times. 

Snow conditions stayed soft and fun to carve yesterday, and when the sun came out it was textbook spring skiing, making for another really enjoyable one on the hill. A few spots were skiing a little slower than previous days, and natural terrain is starting to show some thin cover in places. That said, we’re still holding onto plenty of wall-to-wall runs and solid snow depths to keep the good turns going.

Our grooming team laid down cord across 18 trails overnight, with the exception of Knomes Knoll terrain as that lift won’t be spinning on Monday per usual. Head to Forty Niner and Fools Gold for a good time on classic groomers. Be sure to check the Lifts & Trails Report for the full rundown. 

The Summit Express Quad, Golden Express Quad, Little Pico Triple, and Bonanza Carpet are all scheduled to spin at 9:00 a.m. The Outpost Double remains closed as we look to source and repair a new drive component for the lift. 

It’s about time to think about getting your Spring Pass for unlimited skiing and riding from March 13, 2026, through the very last day of the season. Plus, your Spring Pass is also valid at Killington until it closes for the season

Have a fun day of shredding! 

– Josh

 

Key lifts operate Monday, Thursday, & Friday from 9:00 a.m. - 4:00 p.m. On Saturday & Sunday, key lifts operate from 8:30 a.m. - 4:00 p.m.   

Uphill Travel   

  • Pico Base to the top of Golden Express Quad - Open
  • Pico Base to Pico Summit - Open
  • Summit Glade Route - Open
  • Killington/Pico Mountain Interconnect – Open  

Before heading up the hill, be sure to review our uphill travel policies and grab your Uphill Travel Pass here. All uphill travelers are required to have an Uphill Travel Pass and armband, which can be picked up at any ticket window. Please keep an eye out for grooming equipment and steer clear of mountain ops during non-operating hours.              

Mountain Services   

  • Breakfast: Served every day that lifts are spinning in the Pico Cafeteria (2nd floor) starting at 8:30 a.m. on weekdays and at 8:00 a.m. on weekends/peak dates. Menu includes egg sandwiches and our new signature breakfast burritos.
  • Lunch: Served every day that lifts are spinning in the Pico Cafeteria (2nd floor). Menu includes our full grill menu of burgers, tenders, veggie burgers, and more, plus Caesar salads that are made to order.
  • Last Run Lounge (3rd floor): Open every day that lifts are spinning. Bar service is available from 11:00 a.m. - 5:00 p.m. Menu includes soups, garlic knots, and lite fare, with our pizza station opening up on weekends and busier days. Convenient QR code ordering is available.
  • Guest Services, Rentals, and Pico Sports Shop: Open every day that lifts are spinning from 30 minutes before lifts open until 30 minutes after lifts close.  

Tickets & Lessons   

  • Tickets are on sale at picomountain.com and must be purchased online. My Pico 2025/2026 Season Passes are still on sale!
  • Group lessons are available! Click here for more information. All lessons and rentals must also be booked online.
  • For information on cancellation and change policies, please visit picomountain.com/cancellation   

Pico Fitness Center                              

Visit picomountain.com/fitness to see our fitness class schedule for the winter, plus more details about memberships and pool lane reservations.      

Hours of Operation:   

Monday thru Friday: 6:00 a.m. - 8:00 p.m. 
Saturday & Sunday: 8:00 a.m. - 6:00 p.m.          

We strive to have the most up-to-date and accurate snow report, but errors can happen from time to time. If you have feedback or a correction for us, please share it here.

name: Snow Report 2026-03-08 05:55:44
report:
Good morning, Pico family,

We had an amazing day of skiing and riding here yesterday. The sun popped out for much of the day, softening things up and making for some really fun, carve-friendly turns. It’s really comfortable on the slopes and we’re pretty excited to be making the most of it with every trail to choose from. 

Cloudy skies will start the day before gradually clearing by late morning, with temperatures falling to around the mid-40s by the afternoon. Expect a windy day on the mountain, with west winds of 29 to 38 mph and gusts keeping things feeling a little brisk.

The slopes skied and rode really well yesterday, with light, soft snow that was easy to edge into and was moving quickly. With conditions like this, it’s safe to say spring is starting to make its way onto the hill. The bumps were a joy on Upper Sunset 71 and Birch Glades and we can expect more of that today as the air stays warm and the snow stays soft across the mountain. 

Our grooming team stayed off any trails that didn’t see snowmaking to help preserve the depths there. Runs that are smooth and will provide a great ride are Mid and Lower Pike, Expressway, A Slope, and more. Be sure to check the Lifts & Trails Report for the full rundown. 

The Summit Express Quad, Golden Express Quad, Little Pico Triple, and Bonanza Carpet are all scheduled to spin at 8:30 a.m. The Knomes Knoll Triple will join at 9:00 a.m. The Outpost Double remains closed as we look to source and repair a new drive component for the lift. 

It’s about time to think about getting your Spring Pass for unlimited skiing and riding from March 13, 2026, through the very last day of the season. Plus, your Spring Pass is also valid at Killington until it closes for the season

Have a fun day of shredding! 

– Evan

name: Snow Report 2026-03-07 06:00:34
report:
Good morning, Pico family,

Get ready for another spectacular weekend! 

After an incredible stretch of winter weather, we're in for a taste of spring over the next few days. Temps will be breaking 32 degrees around the time lifts open and by lunchtime, we'll be flirting with 40 degrees as temps will continue to rise into the late afternoon. We had a brief period of light rain and mixed precipitation overnight, but things should clear up early and the majority of the day will be mostly cloudy. Winds are expected to increase to around 40–50 mph at higher elevations, so we'll be keeping an eye on weather conditions in case they impact operations.

Our grooming team went on the prowl last night laying down fresh corduroy on your favorite cruisers. We' have over 20 groomed runs to rip from top to bottom. Mid K.A. and Mid Pike are going to be my favorite groomed runs of the day featuring a mellow pitch and crisp snow, but there's plenty more of that to be found. The grooming team will also be getting Prospector and Panhandler terrain parks back in shape, keeping things playful and creative for riders of all levels.

Conditions this weekend should transition to classic spring skiing and riding once it warms up. The snow surface should start off on the firm side in the morning, with varying conditions across the mountain. Some areas are holding onto packed powder, but we'll most likely see traces of hard pack and loose granular in the morning. Fortunately, the snow will soften to perfection once it warms up. By the afternoon, the trees and bumps will be more enjoyable on trails including Upper Sunset 71 and Birch Glades. Snow conditions will only get better as the weekend progresses, as Sunday looks to be nice and warm, and Monday too with more sunshine.

The Summit Express Quad, Golden Express Quad, Little Pico Triple, Knomes Knoll Triple, and Bonanza Carpet are all scheduled to spin at 8:30 a.m. The Outpost Double remains closed as we look to source and repair a new drive component for the lift. 

It’s about time to think about getting your Spring Pass for unlimited skiing and riding from March 13, 2026, through the very last day of the season. Plus, your Spring Pass is also valid at Killington until it closes for the season

Have a great weekend! 

– Evan

Key lifts operate Monday, Thursday, & Friday from 9:00 a.m. - 4:00 p.m. On Saturday & Sunday, key lifts operate from 8:30 a.m. - 4:00 p.m.

The Summit Express Quad, Golden Express Quad, Little Pico Triple, Knomes Knoll Triple, and Bonanza Carpet are all scheduled to spin at 8:30 a.m. The Outpost Double remains closed as we look to source and repair a new drive component for the lift.

name: Snow Report 2026-03-06 06:04:21
report:
Good morning, Pico family,

It was another sunny and pleasant day on the mountain with great snow to shred on Thursday.  It feels good to be back on snow as we make our towards another great weekend on the slopes.

An inch of snow moved in last night along with sleet and freezing rain. There's another chance of snow early today, then mostly cloudy with highs around 29°F at the base and 33°F at the top, and a breezy southeast wind should keep things feeling like midwinter. 

Our grooming team went after the same plan with Knomes Knoll terrain added back into the mix, including The Draw, Ace of Clubs, and Tripe Slope. Ace of Clubs should be skiing and & riding nicely as well as Fools Gold after receiving fresh corduroy. Due to the interesting weather last night, conditions may vary across the mountain from packed powder to firm snow. Higher elevations received more snow overnight and should resemble mid winter conditions, while lower elevations have traces of crust, especially on natural terrain. Ski Patrol will be monitoring the hill this morning, so be sure to check the Lifts & Trails Report for all the latest updates.

The Summit Express Quad, Golden Express Quad, Little Pico Triple, Knomes Knoll Triple, and Bonanza Carpet will all be spinning at 9:00 a.m. 

The 2026 Spring Pass is on sale for unlimited skiing and riding from March 13, 2026, through the very last day of the season at both Pico and Killington. Pico, Midweek and Ikon Pass holders can upgrade at any time for just $199. March 12 is the last day to score the best price at $379. This winter is far from finished. 

Have a good Friday! 

– Evan

name: Snow Report 2026-03-05 16:40:40
report:
Good evening, Pico family,

It was another sunny and pleasant day on the mountain with great snow to shred. After a couple days off, it feels good to be back open and shredding here.

Snow moves in tonight with a period of sleet mixed in, potentially brining an inch or two of accumulation by morning. Tomorrow is mostly cloudy with a slight chance of lingering snow early, highs around 29°F at the base and 33°F at the top, and a breezy southeast wind to keep things feeling like midwinter. 

Our grooming team is after the same plan with Knomes Knoll terrain added back into the mix, including The Draw, Ace of Clubs, and Tripe Slope. Ace of Clubs should be skiing and & riding wonderfully as well as Fools Gold after receiving fresh corduroy. 

The Summit Express Quad, Golden Express Quad, Little Pico Triple, Knomes Knoll Triple, and Bonanza Carpet will all be spinning at 9:00 a.m. 

It’s about time to think about getting your Spring Pass for unlimited skiing and riding from March 13, 2026, through the very last day of the season. Plus, your Spring Pass is also valid at Killington until it closes for the season

Have a fun Friday! 

– Evan

Key lifts operate Monday, Thursday, & Friday from 9:00 a.m. - 4:00 p.m. On Saturday & Sunday, key lifts operate from 8:30 a.m. - 4:00 p.m.   

Uphill Travel   

  • Pico Base to the top of Golden Express Quad - Open
  • Pico Base to Pico Summit - Open
  • Summit Glade Route - Open
  • Killington/Pico Mountain Interconnect – Open  

Before heading up the hill, be sure to review our uphill travel policies and grab your Uphill Travel Pass here. All uphill travelers are required to have an Uphill Travel Pass and armband, which can be picked up at any ticket window. Please keep an eye out for grooming equipment and steer clear of mountain ops during non-operating hours.              

Mountain Services   

  • Breakfast: Served every day that lifts are spinning in the Pico Cafeteria (2nd floor) starting at 8:30 a.m. on weekdays and at 8:00 a.m. on weekends/peak dates. Menu includes egg sandwiches and our new signature breakfast burritos.
  • Lunch: Served every day that lifts are spinning in the Pico Cafeteria (2nd floor). Menu includes our full grill menu of burgers, tenders, veggie burgers, and more, plus Caesar salads that are made to order.
  • Last Run Lounge (3rd floor): Open every day that lifts are spinning. Bar service is available from 11:00 a.m. - 5:00 p.m. Menu includes soups, garlic knots, and lite fare, with our pizza station opening up on weekends and busier days. Convenient QR code ordering is available.
  • Guest Services, Rentals, and Pico Sports Shop: Open every day that lifts are spinning from 30 minutes before lifts open until 30 minutes after lifts close.  

Tickets & Lessons   

  • Tickets are on sale at picomountain.com and must be purchased online. My Pico 2025/2026 Season Passes are still on sale!
  • Group lessons are available! Click here for more information. All lessons and rentals must also be booked online.
  • For information on cancellation and change policies, please visit picomountain.com/cancellation   

Pico Fitness Center                              

Visit picomountain.com/fitness to see our fitness class schedule for the winter, plus more details about memberships and pool lane reservations.      

Hours of Operation:   

Monday thru Friday: 6:00 a.m. - 8:00 p.m. 
Saturday & Sunday: 8:00 a.m. - 6:00 p.m.          

We strive to have the most up-to-date and accurate snow report, but errors can happen from time to time. If you have feedback or a correction for us, please share it here.

name: Snow Report 2026-03-05 05:57:20
report:
Good morning, Pico family,

That 4 inches of fresh snow was just what we needed and it’s shaping up to be a great day back on the mountain! Today brings a mix of sun and clouds, with inverted highs of 37 at the peak and 35 at the base. Temperatures will be sliding down to around 25 degrees by late afternoon. A light northwest breeze in the morning will shift to the east as the day rolls on, keeping things crisp while you stack those afternoon laps.

Our grooming team added A Slope, B Slope, Lower Giant Killer, Lower Sunset 71, and Bonanza to the grooming plan overnight. Get on Lower Giant Killer while that trail is still able to see smooth corduroy and maybe a lap on Forty Niner for an early morning cruise on fresh cord. 

The Summit Express Quad, Golden Express Quad, Little Pico Triple, and Bonanza Carpet will all be spinning at 9:00 a.m. 

With this nice weather in the air, it's time to think about getting your Spring Pass for unlimited skiing and riding from March 13, 2026, through the very last day of the season. Plus, your Spring Pass is also valid at Killington until it closes for the season

We’re back to shredding the hill!

name: Snow Report 2026-03-04 16:38:23
report:
Hey, Pico family,

Those 4 inches of fresh snow were just what we needed and it’s shaping up to be a great day back on the mountain! Thursday brings a mix of sun and clouds, with inverted highs of 37 at the peak and 35 at the base. Temperatures will be sliding down to around 25 degrees by late afternoon. A light northwest breeze in the morning will shift to the east as the day rolls on, keeping things crisp while you stack those afternoon laps.

The slopes were skiing beautifully today, with a mix of packed powder and soft snow that only got better as the sun came out. Our grooming team is adding A Slope, B Slope, Lower Giant Killer, and Bonanza to the groomed trails tonight. Get on Lower Giant Killer while that trail is still able to see smooth corduroy and maybe a lap on Forty Niner for a early morning cruise on fresh cord. 

The Summit Express Quad, Golden Express Quad, Little Pico Triple, and Bonanza Carpet will all be spinning at 9:00 a.m. 

With this nice weather in the air, it's time to think about getting your Spring Pass for unlimited skiing and riding from March 13, 2026, through the very last day of the season. Plus, your Spring Pass is also valid at Killington until it closes for the season

We’re back to shredding the hill tomorrow, hooray! 

– Evan
